7/ Mercado Libre

$MELI Latin American platform includes e-commerce, logistics, payments, credit, and advertising, making it easy to use for hundreds of millions of users.

Built on a highly scalable but asset-intensive technology base, the company combines a marketplace model with its own logistics and payments systems. This structure supports efficient growth while turning its high fixed costs into a strong competitive advantage.

With $59.6B in platform sales and $253B in payment volume, Mercado Libre has become the core infrastructure for digital commerce in the region. It continues to benefit from long-term growth in online shopping, digital payments, and broader financial adoption.

Furthermore, contrary to popular belief, at maturity, e-commerce penetration in these markets will be even higher than in developed markets.

This is because:
1) Lower-income people tend to price-match more
2) There are fewer convenient brick-and-mortar commerce options. 

You don't have as many auto parts shops in a 20K town in Brazil as you do in the US. So a Brazilian living there is more likely to order online than drive 1H to the nearest large auto parts shop to get the specific part he needs.

Moreover, a large portion of the population in South America remains underbanked or unbanked, limiting access to traditional financial services. Mercado Libre addresses this gap through Mercado Pago, which provides digital payments, credit, and financial tools. This drives the flywheel, creating more sales.

"Mercado Libre"

Porque $MELI aumenta un 50% su inversión en Brasil para expandir logística y capturar el crecimiento del ecommerce. 

Es su mercado más importante y donde se juega gran parte de su valuación.

Brasil ya representa más del 50% del negocio de MercadoLibre. Por eso, la compañía planea invertir 57.000 millones de reales (~USD 11.000M) en 2026, enfocados principalmente en logística, tecnología y expansión operativa.

El plan incluye abrir 14 nuevos centros de distribución, alcanzando un total de 42 CD en el país, lo que implica un crecimiento del 50% interanual. La apuesta tiende a reducir tiempos de entrega y mejorar la experiencia del usuario para consolidar su liderazgo frente a Amazon.

El ecommerce en Brasil sigue creciendo a doble dígito, y la logística es el principal diferencial competitivo.

“Mercado Libre”

Porque $MELI cotiza cerca de sus valuaciones más bajas de los últimos años, aunque sigue mostrando un crecimiento muy fuerte: +39% en 2025 y proyectando otro año en torno al 30-35%.

De hecho, acumula 27 trimestres seguidos creciendo por encima del 30%.

Los analistas siguen viendo upside incluso en el escenario más conservador.

En 2025 alcanzó ingresos de USD 28.900 millones (+39% interanual), con algunos trimestres que llegaron a +45%.

El mercado castigó la acción por márgenes más ajustados y ganancias por debajo de lo esperado, mientras el negocio continúa acelerando.

El gran motor sigue siendo Mercado Pago, con crédito, pagos y servicios financieros expandiéndose incluso más rápido que el e-commerce.

Brasil y México siguen liderando el volumen, impulsados por logística y crédito en todo el ecosistema.

Hoy la acción está unos -30% por debajo de sus máximos históricos, con targets de analistas bastante más altos para el mediano plazo.

MercadoLibre $MELI has crossed its 200-week moving average for the 9th major time. 

Historically, this specific trendline has acted as a generational buy signal for long term investors. While this is the 9th distinct period of testing this level, the stock has only seen roughly 11–12 specific weekly closes at or below this line since its 2007 IPO. 

In terms of major cyclical buying opportunities, we are currently witnessing the 9th major test of this multi-decade trend.

Across the previous eight instances where $MELI tested this level, the average 1-year forward return was 52%. 

There has been only one negative 1-year return following a 200-week MA touch: 

• 2008 Global Financial Crisis (-18.7%)

In every other instance:

• 2011 European Debt Crisis / LatAm inflation (+29.4%)

• 2014 Brazil economic recession (+31.2%), 

• 2016 Argentina currency devaluation (+70.4%) 

• 2018 Fed rate hike scare (+68.4%),

• 2020 Covid19 pandemic lows (+114.5%)

• 2022 Tech bear market / high inflation  (+72.1%)

• 2024 Brazil Tax / margin concerns (+51.3%)

J&T Global on $MELI. Some interesting insights. With rising U.S. tariff uncertainties and saturated Southeast Asian markets, Chinese manufacturers are actively looking for new demand centers. MELI is positioning itself as the primary toll bridge for these merchants to access Brazil, Mexico, and the broader LatAm market. Because of their continental logistics network they offer Chinese sellers a unique value proposition. 

The J&T executive confirms that MELI's cross-border and local volume is a massive driver for J&T's own growth in New Markets (specifically Brazil and Mexico). By handling the logistics for these incoming Chinese goods, MELI and partners like J&T are creating a flywheel: cheaper Chinese goods lead to higher transaction volumes, which increases the total parcel count, driving down unit logistics costs and ultimately boosting MELI's GMV and commerce margins.

They are successfully onboarding high volumes of Chinese merchants, which will likely serve as a massive catalyst for MELI's cross-border GMV growth in 2026.

“We know that Mercado Libre is the biggest market, biggest company in Latin America. And at the current stage, we are now further focusing on the development. So at the same time, in Mercado Libre, this is actually the biggest company in Latin America before we enter into the market. So while we actually further increase our investment there in Latin America, this is actually bringing up a lot of other sellers to sell their products in Latin America. And you can see that there are more merchants now operating on Mercado Libre, which is going to be a very good thing driven by our performance. So for instance, the automotive in China, some of the like phone case players are performing quite well. But when they actually enter the Southeast Asian market, they are going to be working with Mercado Libre. And at the current stage, this is already the case. And next, Mercado Libre is now increasing their investments in China. In December, I think that they have opened a dedicated account in China… So with that particular strength, we are seeing a very good performance. And at the same time, I think that they have a very big room for further improvement. We always believe that in the future, Mercado Libre will be bringing with us more contributions from the increase of the number of parcels in Latin America.”

Casas Bahia on their partnership with $MELI:

“It helps with stock turn. It helps us ensure a healthier inventory, and it captures opportunities that sometimes we would not be able to reach because we're talking about a very specific customer from a smaller category because we're always looking at the 80-20 ratio. The tool encourage competition between sellers. You have a lot of visibility on the strategy from different players in the digital market, and that helps us adjust our strategy, both at MercadoLibre and in our own channel. So it helps us keep our competitiveness with more access to detail, which is important and positive. And another positive surprise was to see that some categories that are relevant for the company, but many times are not mentioned, one example being tires, large or bulky products that we sell at a relevant volume, but they are not the core of what we do. Well, somehow it is core because we have a different logistics for bulky products. And these sales also grew in a very significant way, showing that there is a potential for having dedicated people for these strategic items for the company, relevant items for the company that could have a bigger impact in our P&L.”

💸El monto de 57 mil millones de reales (casi USD 11.000 millones) supone un incremento del 50% en comparación con la inversión de 38.000 millones de reales realizada en 2025.
